item.title: A Restricted Boltzmann Machine in R
item.author: kmckee90@gmail.com (Kevin McKee)
item.description: github source for this project I’ve taken an interest in a succession of binary network models: the hopfield network, Boltzmann Machine, the Restricted Boltzmann Machine (RBM), and the Helmholtz Machine. There isn’t much implementation of these in R, but I did find this tutorial. Each model in some way learns the joint probability distribution of many binary variables using binary hidden nodes. The difference between the RBM and the Helmholtz machine is the former uses a kind of Gibbs sampling of the hidden states to compute gradients of node weights, whereas the ladder is a generative network that learns by “dreaming” fake data during the sleep phase of the wake-sleep algorithm.

item.title: Painting a harpsichord lid
item.author: kmckee90@gmail.com (Kevin McKee)
item.description: Some process photos from my painting on a harpsichord lid. (I’m using this post to figure out getting images into blog posts and such.) I painted this harpsichord lid for a commission in 2016. I started with a photoshop mockup: The first stages of the actual painting are interesting to look back on because they show how fundamental color and movement are to the composition: Final result:

item.title: About me
item.author: kmckee90@gmail.com (Kevin McKee)
item.description: I live in Nelson County, Virginia, on the side of a mountain with my wife, Linnea, a cat, and a dog. In my free time I do things with the surrounding land like clearing hiking trails, hunting for edible mushrooms, and stargazing. Job title: Postdoctoral Associate Department of Statistics Center for Biostatistics and Health Data Science Virginia Tech Curriculum Vitae

item.title: Code
item.author: kmckee90@gmail.com (Kevin McKee)
item.description: All coding projects can be found on github. The links to posts for each project should show up under “See Also” on this page. Some of my projects are completely lacking in annotations, full of commented out zombie code, and generally messy. I’ll be in the process of cleaning them up as I write about them.

item.title: Publications
item.author: kmckee90@gmail.com (Kevin McKee)
item.description: McKee, K. L., (2021) Hierarchical Biometrical Genetic Analysis of Dynamics. Behavior Genetics (in press) McKee, K. L., Crandell, I. C., Hanlon, A. L. (2020) US County-Level Social Distancing and Policy Impact: A Dynamical Systems Model. Journal of Medical Internet Research https://doi.org/10.2196/23902 McKee, K. L., Russell, M., Mennis, J., Mason, M., & Neale, M. C. (2019). Emotion Regulation Dynamics Predict Substance Use in High-Risk Adolescents. Addictive Behaviors https://doi.org/10.1016/j.addbeh.2020.106374 McKee, K. L., Phenotype Extraction: Estimation and Biometrical Genetic Analysis of Individual Dynamics, Virginia Commonwealth University.
